title: Western Blot Sample Prep- Whole Drosophila Embryos
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"btujc","text":"It is important to plan the timing of the embryo collection in advance. To select a specific genotype, be sure to cross in the selectable balancer that will allow you to distinguish mutants from non-mutants. To prevent the collection of unfertilized animals into sample, it is helpful to select animals that have developed far enough through embryogenesis that auto-fluorescence of the gut can be observed. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bumaj","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fkbq8","text":"Example:To collect stage 17 Mcr[P1] animals: Setup a four hour collection and age for 17 hours at 25 degrees Celsius. For homozygous mutants, collect non-YFP animals and select only animals that have auto-fluorescence in the gut.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Collect 100 embryos and place into eppendorf tube (1.5ml) containing 50ul of embryo wash undetermined
Add 3 drops of bleach and let sit for 3 mins. undetermined 00:03:00
Raise volume to 1.5ml using dH2O Spin down for 1min undetermined 00:01:00
Remove solution Add 1.5ml of dH2O Spin down 1min 00:01:00
Remove solution Add 1.5ml of dH2O Spin down 1 min undetermined 00:01:00
Remove solution with micropipette Add 50ul of 1X PBS undetermined
Boil for 5 mins at 98 degrees C 00:05:00
creator: Sonia M Hall
